name: Kensington SD5700T Thunderbolt 4 Docking Station
shortDescription: The Kensington SD5700T is a reliable hybrid Thunderbolt 4 dock supporting both Mac and Windows with dual 4K, 90W charging, and Kensington's enterprise-grade build quality at $169.
useCases: Hybrid Mac/Windows environments with a single dock
Dual 4K monitor setup on any Thunderbolt 4 host
90W laptop charging for MacBook Air and 14" MacBook Pro
Enterprise desk setups requiring reliable, warranty-backed hardware
pros: Best value in the roundup at $169 — lowest price for a certified TB4 dock
True hybrid Mac/Windows compatibility with no driver quirks
Kensington enterprise build quality — designed for 8+ hour daily use
cons: Fewer ports than CalDigit TS4 or Plugable TBT4-UD5
Basic industrial design compared to CalDigit or Anker
verdict: The Kensington SD5700T is the best value Thunderbolt 4 dock for users who want reliability over port count. At $169, it undercuts the competition while delivering everything most users need: dual 4K, 90W charging, Ethernet, and true plug-and-play on both Mac and Windows. If you need 2.5GbE or more USB ports, step up to the Plugable TBT4-UD5. Otherwise, this is the easy budget recommendation.

faq.question: Is the Kensington SD5700T compatible with Mac Mini M4?
faq.answer: Yes. The SD5700T connects to Mac Mini M4 via Thunderbolt 4 and delivers dual 4K displays, USB-A ports, Ethernet, and pass-through power. It's one of the cleanest plug-and-play experiences on macOS.

faq.question: Does the SD5700T work with Windows 11 mini PCs?
faq.answer: Yes. Kensington explicitly supports Windows 11 and has tested compatibility with major mini PC brands. On Thunderbolt 4 certified Windows mini PCs, all ports work immediately without driver installation.

notFor: Heavy USB peripheral users needing 12+ ports
entityHeadline: Kensington SD5700T Review: Best Value Thunderbolt 4 Dock for Mac and Windows
